headlines.title: Man taken to the hospital after being stabbed in San Francisco
headlines.description: A man was taken to the hospital after being stabbed in San Francisco Sunday afternoon, according to the fire department. Authorities said the incident occurred on 19th and Mission streets at around 4 p.m. The victim was taken to the hospital with serious injuries. The incident took place near the area of the city’s annual Carnaval parade.

headlines.title: 2 people found dead inside vehicle in San Jose State's parking garage
headlines.description: Two people were found dead inside a vehicle in a SJSU parking garage Saturday afternoon, according to officials. The SJSU police said they responded to a report of two people unresponsive in a vehicle in the West Garage at 350 S. 4th St. just after 2 p.m. Authorities said they found a man and a woman unresponsive. Firefighters administered CPR to stabilize them, but they were pronounced dead at 2:20 p.m., according to officials. Police said the pair was not affiliated with the school and added this is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: 2 people hurt after AC Transit bus crashes into building in Oakland
headlines.description: Two people were hurt and an AC Transit bus driver was rescued following a crash in Oakland, the fire department said on Sunday. The fire department reported the crash occurred at around 11:45 p.m. Authorities with AC Transit said, however, the crash happened just after midnight. According to AC Transit, a Toyota Camry was traveling north on 98th Avenue towards I-580 when it reportedly ran a red light and collided with the bus, causing the bus to leave the roadway and hit the exterior of a building at 98th Avenue and MacArthur Boulevard. AC Transit also said an officer reportedly witnessed the Camry’s driver running the red light. Authorities said two people were injured, and the bus driver was trapped for a short time until they were eventually rescued. Officials said there was a request for board up, but the building suffered minor damage.

headlines.title: Thousands attend the Carnaval San Francisco Grand Parade
headlines.description: Thousands of people filled the Mission District Sunday for the Carnaval San Francisco Grand Parade. It’s the culmination of the annual two-day multicultural celebration honoring the diverse traditions of Latin America and the Caribbean. The mission district came alive for the 48th Annual Carnaval Parade. People lined up early for the best spots along the 20-block parade route. Mia came in from Chico to the city by the Bay. “The vibes are immaculate everyone is so happy and cheering all the cars, we’re all waiting for the dancers as well,” Mia said. Organizers say the parade featured than 70 continents and 5,000 artists representing the heritage of countries including Brazil, Mexico, Trinidad, Tobago, Puerto Rico and beyond. “I’m a native, this is a tradition of the Latin culture here in San Francisco, we’re all united as one,” Juanita said. Juanita says it’s a can’t miss celebration. “To celebrate the happiness that we have in our culture, everyone is so diverse everyone loves to be here and represents themselves,” she said. This year’s theme was La Copa del Pueblo (The People’s Cup), in honor of the World Cup coming to the Bay Area this summer. Neighbors, friends and family shared in community and culture.

headlines.title: San Francisco club spurs debate over access to public tennis courts
headlines.description: Some vocal detractors in San Francisco’s Richmond District have been calling out a private tennis club for occupying public courts. Neighbors said, in the grand scheme of things, not getting a tennis court is far from the biggest problem in the city, but it has prompted a debate about who should be able to access public resources. This all stems from a recent Reddit post, and commenters called out the Rossi Racquet Club for taking up space at the public tennis courts at Rossi Park in the Richmond District. According to the club’s MeetUp activities page, the group takes suggested donations of $5. Critics say they take up highly sought-after space on the public, first-come-first serve courts. The park has three tennis courts, one can be reserved in advance for $7.50, and the other two can be accessed on a first-come, first-served basis. The city also has its own rules for play at public tennis courts. In an interview with the San Francisco Standard, the club’s founder pushed back, saying that people don’t have to pay or be part of the group to join and that his group only plays from 8 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. on weekends. He told the Standard “It’s all drama, we don’t appreciate the internet witch hunt.” But other tennis players NBC Bay Area spoke with disagree. “I think it’s unfair to the local tennis players who are out here trying to have a good time and trying to have easy access to tennis,” said San Francisco resident Calvin Brown, who was playing on the courts Sunday. A few local tennis players also told NBC Bay Area they believe members of this club should have access to the courts just like any other member of the public. NBC Bay Area reached out to the club’s founder but was unable to contact him. NBC Bay Area also reached out to the San Francisco Parks Department for comment

headlines.title: 15-year-old suspect arrested in stabbing of man after graduation in Daly City
headlines.description: A 15-year-old suspect was arrested in connection with the stabbing of a 21-year-old man following a graduation ceremony in Daly City on Friday, authorities said on Saturday. The Daly City Police Department said a fight involving multiple people took place on Westmoor High School campus, following the graduation ceremony at approximately 4:30 p.m. They added that the victim sustained non-life-threatening injuries and was taken to the hospital. The school district initially reported several suspects were arrested, but the police department later said a 15-year-old suspect had been arrested on suspicion of assault with a deadly weapon and did not mention any other arrests. The graduation ceremony was scheduled to begin at 2:30 p.m. Friday. Videos of this incident spread widely on social media. In the videos, a group of people in sweatshirts, with their faces covered, could be seen fighting at the school’s basketball courts. Many students in graduation robes could be seen nearby. One of the people in the fight could be seen stabbing another, leaving that victim bloodied. Police officers were then seen rushing in to intervene. In a statement, Westmoor High School’s Principal Victor Zou said that the victim has no relationship to the school district. “My thoughts are with all those impacted by this incident,” Zou said. “The safety and well-being of our students, families, staff, and community remains my highest priority, as well as our district’s.” It is unclear if the suspect is connected to the school. “I was here yesterday when I saw the police, they were surrounded — with the yellow tape around, they were collecting evidence,” said Tony Liu, a neighbor who has lived in Daly City for three decades. “The next thing I knew, it was [all] over the news, somebody got stabbed, surprisingly it was at a high school graduation, which shouldn’t happen,” Liu continued. Another neighbor, Josue Mejia, said his brother showed him the videos of the fight on social media. “There’s like videos all around about it, you know,” Mejia said. “And we wondered, like, why? That’s the main question.” Neighbors described the area near the school as relatively quiet, adding that they were alarmed to hear about the stabbing, especially on graduation day. “I was like, surprised you know? We never expect these kinds of things in school,” Mejia said. “You expect like kids to be protected, ” he continued. Daly City Police were on campus Saturday, keeping an eye out. The police department is still actively investigating this case. Police are asking for anyone with information about this incident to contact their Investigations Division at (650) 991-816.

headlines.title: Apparent fake threat closes San Jose's Happy Hollow Zoo, police say
headlines.description: An apparent fake threat closed San Jose’s Happy Hollow Park and Zoo on Saturday, according to the police department. Police said they responded to a “suspicious circumstance” at the zoo at around 10:20 a.m. There, they were made aware of a possible swatting call in regard to a threat involving explosives. Authorities said the area was evacuated out of abundance of caution. Police said officers did not find anything suspicious inside the park, but added staff advised the park will remain closed for the rest of the day.

headlines.title: Santa Clara County opens vote centers ahead of June 2 primary
headlines.description: California’s June 2 primary election is less than two weeks away, and Santa Clara County’s Registrar of Voters already opened a number of vote centers for people to drop off their mail ballots, vote in person or get assistance. San Jose Mayor Matt Mahan, who is a candidate for governor, was among the first to casting their ballots in San Jose. “Just voted with my family, it’s the first person day of person voting, you can vote in person in California between now and election day, just make sure you get you ballots in by June 2,” he said. Santa Clara County opened up 24 vote centers Saturday, with dozens more coming soon. Matt Moreles with the country’s registrar of voters says all the vote centers will have staff available to answer any questions. So far, the county has received about 80,000 ballots back. Christie Smith has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: SF high school baseball coaches under investigation for alleged misconduct
headlines.description: Three baseball coaches at Lowell High School were relieved of their duties and are under investigation for alleged misconduct, the San Francisco Unified School District confirmed Saturday. The district did not elaborate on what exactly led to the disciplinary action and the investigation. But the Chronicle is citing allegations of racial misconduct involving players. The district also released a statement saying in part, “Discriminatory behavior and hate speech have no place in our schools or athletic settings.”

headlines.title: Firefighters tackle fire at abandoned school in Vallejo
headlines.description: An abandoned school in Vallejo was destroyed by flames early Saturday morning, according to the fire department. Fire officials said they responded to a fire at the abandoned Grant School located at 5th and Cherry streets, next door to the fire department, at around 1:24 a.m. There, firefighters found the building engulfed in flames. As of 8:30 a.m., the fire department reported crews were able to contain the fire, but the building was still smoldering with fire burning in some enclosed spaces in portions of the school. Authorities said the school has been closed for several years. They add no injuries were reported. Officials said the cause is under investigation, but there was no electricity or gas in the building, which appears to have been occupied by “unauthorized” residents.

headlines.title: Fairfield police announce outside investigation of incident involving officer and student
headlines.description: On Friday, Fairfield police announced the officer involved in a incident at Fairfield High School on Wednesday has been “administratively reassigned in the department” as an outside organization conducts an investigation. Police said a school resource officer (SRO) working at Fairfield High School on Wednesday asked for emergency assistance after responding to a fight. According to authorities, several officers responded to the scene, and one of them assisted the SRO with the arrest of a young man. The moment was captured on camera, and the video went viral. The footage, which shows what it seemed like the officer hitting the student in the face, sparked outrage among the community in Fairfield and around Northen California. On Thursday, the police department released its own videos showing different angles of the situation. On Friday, Fairfield police chief announced the incident will be now investigated independently by an outside organization. Alyssa Goard has the full report in the video player above.

headlines.title: Authorities investigate stabbing after graduation ceremony in Daly City
headlines.description: Authorities are investigating a stabbing that ocurred following a graduation ceremony at Westmoor High School on Friday, according to the school. Officials said the stabbing occurred on campus following the ceremony, which was scheduled to begin at 2:30 p.m. They said police and emergency personnel responded and remain on site as they continue their investigation. School authorities said they are cooperating with law enforcement.
